items.title: 'Confusopoly' blamed as power bill changes surprise customers

children.content: Some electricity users have been hit with higher charges, despite all the talk of the lower default market offers. Retailers say a rebalancing of supply and usage charges is the cause, but there are signs some are clawing back profit by raising their cheapest plans.

gallery.altText: A graphic image of a power plug overlaid on a house and electricity bill

thumbnail.altText: A graphic image of a power plug overlaid on a house and electricity bill

items.title: Self-managed super sector blindsided by Labor–Greens deal on tax package

children.content: A ban on self-managed super funds borrowing money to buy housing has blindsided the sector, which says Labor should instead target "unscrupulous" operators spruiking property as an investment.

image.altText: Two suited men walk towards lecterns.

items.title: Minister says Australian housing market experiencing a 'correction'

children.content: Housing Minister Clare O'Neil says Australia's housing market is experiencing a correction following COVID amid low auction clearance rates in major capital cities. Follow live.

image.altText: Lady standing in front of media

items.title: 'I love you': Sydney shark attack survivor's first words to family

children.content: A Sydney mother mauled by a shark at Coogee Beach almost a fortnight ago has spoken her first words after briefly waking from an induced coma.

image.altText: Sydney mother and Coogee local, Leah Stewart, who was mauled by a shark holds a baby

items.title: US Senate calls for Trump to end Iran war in bipartisan vote

children.content: It is the 10th time the Senate has tried to stop the war, and the outcome, on a vote of 50–48, is a stunning turnaround from past efforts.

image.altText: A group of politicians surround a podium with hands raised.

images.altText: Harry Kane sinks to his knees and puts his head in his hands
Harry Kane sits on the grass and looks up at the ref to complain

articles.title: Ronaldo roars back to life, with his moments off the ball the most telling

children.content: Cristiano Ronaldo came into Portugal’s second World Cup group game under a firestorm of criticism. He answered with two goals, and important signs he is willing to be a team player.

image.altText: Cristiano Ronaldo smirks in the tunnel before the game

children.content: England is held to a frustrating 0-0 draw by a resolute Ghana after Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice in a 5-0 Portugal win over Uzbekistan. Follow live.

items.title: England frustrated by resolute Ghana, Ronaldo makes a statement

updates.title: FT: England held by resolute Ghana to 0-0 draw
HT: England held by ultra-defensive Ghana

items.title: Construction company hit by alleged multi-million-dollar insider fraud

children.content: Queensland police have uncovered an alleged industrial espionage operation that caused major construction company Shadforth's confidential data to be leaked to competitors.

image.altText: ute in front of building

items.title: Experts ask why triple murderer was granted bail before deadly spree

children.content: Sophie Quinn sought police protection from her former partner. Less than two months later he murdered her and two others during a shooting spree at Lake Cargelligo.

image.altText: A sign of a police sign in blue, and a courthouse sign for Lake Cargelligo.

items.title: Port Moresby's water supply network showing 'signs of imminent failure'

children.content: A decades-old pipeline supplying water to nearly 1 million people in Papua New Guinea's capital is at risk of collapsing without urgent repairs, threatening to cut off more than half of the city's water supply.

image.altText: Water bursts from two points in a long concrete flume.

items.title: Limits for 'silent killer' chemical exposure on hold despite advice

children.content: A majority of state and territory governments have put stricter workplace exposure limits on hold for nine hazardous chemicals.

image.altText: A worker in hi-vis clothing walks across a sandy construction site with machinery and Fiona Stanley Hospital in the background.

items.title: IVF add-ons are common in Australia, but there's no proof most work

children.content: Experts urge clinics and specialists to "carefully consider" offering IVF add-on treatments as they can be seen as "implicit endorsement of benefit" when there's little evidence most work.

gallery.altText: A person wearing a teal cap looking at a screen with a magnified embryo picture
A composite image of a woman smiling with a needle and a woman taking a selfie.

thumbnail.altText: A person wearing a teal cap looking at a screen with a magnified embryo picture
A composite image of a woman smiling with a needle and a woman taking a selfie.

items.title: Australians financially supporting family in Lebanon say they have no other option

image.altText: A photo of Sue (Far right) with her family pictured behind them in Lebanon.

children.content: Lebanese Australians, who have spent thousands of dollars to help their loved ones survive a two-and-a-half-year war, bear a financial burden that also comes with an emotional toll.

items.title: Outgoing British PM holds secret meeting with likely successor

children.content: Sir Keir Starmer and the man who has essentially forced him to resign as prime minister, Andy Burnham, have held their first face-to-face meeting.

image.altText: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer looks sideways.

items.title: ASX to rise ahead of inflation figures, despite Wall Street's tech sell-off

children.content: The Australian share market is expected to rise despite a major sell-off across US tech, AI and semiconductor stocks, which resulted in steep losses on Wall Street. The ABS will release its May inflation data, which will be impacted by sharp falls in petrol and diesel prices. Follow live.

image.altText: The ASX building exterior.

items.title: Gardening Australia star discovered plants unknown to science but couldn't be his true self

children.content: Gardening Australia star Jerry Coleby-Williams hid his identity on TV for 26 years, but has never been ashamed of being a gay man.

gallery.altText: Jerry Coleby-Williams stands outside smiling, holding up a hanging pot overflowing with cherry tomatoes

items.title: Siri Hustvedt felt her husband's ghost. She wrote a book about it

children.content: The author of What I Loved has published a moving memoir drawing on letters and journals documenting the last days of Paul Auster, her partner in life and writing.

image.altText: An old photo of a young couple: a blonde woman in a singlet and shorts, and a dark-haired man in a short-sleeved shirt.

items.title: Cheap and easy ways to heat the human instead of the home

children.content: Heating your whole house — or even a room — can be expensive in the depths of winter. Keeping the humans inside it warm is often cheaper and more efficient.

image.altText: A close up of the hand of a person with black painted nails, wearing a cream wooly jumper, holding a cup of tea.

items.title: The 'uncontrolled' police department behind fatal shooting of Perth girl

children.content: The grandfather of Australian schoolgirl Hania Ahmed recounts the aftermath of her fatal shooting, as a senior Pakistani lawyer takes aim at the "uncontrolled" police department at the centre of her death.

Mazhar sitting down with a sad, troubled expression.

items.title: 'Punched in the guts': Sacked academics take uni to Fair Work Commission

image.altText: A middle aged woman with short white hair sitting in a loungeroom

children.content: Two senior academics from Macquarie University allege they were targeted for redundancy because of their union activity.

items.title: UN inquiry accuses Israel of deliberately targeting and killing Palestinian children

children.content: The Israeli government is condemning the inquiry and rejecting its findings, labelling the United Nations report "deeply flawed" and full of "errors and distortions" designed to push a "politically driven anti-Israel narrative".

image.altText: A young child looking through a makeshift tent's window.

items.title: Australia launches world-first suicide prevention approach

children.content: Policymakers, mental health experts and those working on the front line will use the National Suicide Prevention Outcomes Map to gauge what services are working to prevent people from reaching critical crisis points.

image.altText: Jo Riley smiles while in a garden. She is wearing a colourful top and a necklace.

items.title: Ex-CEO vindicated in $2.3 million court ruling against space firm

children.content: Once a promising space start-up chief executive, Carley Scott was terminated from her job and left millions out of pocket. Now she's finally found vindication, with a court ruling in her favour.

image.altText: A woman with a brown ponytail wearing hi-vis smiles at a worksite

items.title: At least 40 drownings reported in France as Europe swelters

children.content: Schools are being ordered to close in the UK and railways are urging against non-essential travel across the continent as record-breaking heat takes hold.

image.altText: A construction worker wearing high-visibility clothing drinking from a water bottle.

items.title: 'I get a lot of anxiety': Borrowers caught out with high-interest car loans

children.content: A new report warns customers about traps they face when taking out a car loan, including high-interest rates and fees.

gallery.altText: A woman with a leg injury sits in the front seat of her car holding the steering wheel.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with a leg injury sits in the front seat of her car holding the steering wheel.

items.title: Five key takeaways from Crisafulli's second budget

children.content: This year's Queensland budget includes no "sugar hits" when it comes to cost of living, a big interest repayment bill and plenty of construction projects in the pipeline.

image.altText: Two men in suits standing at podiums with flags on either side.

items.title: Budget offers short-term sugar hit but there could be bitterness to come

children.content: Faced with rising interest rates, high grocery prices and bloated bills, the cost-of-living help announced in Tuesday's budget will only make a minor dent in household budgets, Nick Dole writes.

image.altText: photo of a hand with empty cup of coffee and cash on a wooden table

avatar.alt: ABC reporter Nick Dole looking straight at the camera with a grey background

items.title: Whistleblower doctors net $9.9m in NSW budget for robotic surgery

children.content: A long-running campaign to secure robotic surgery in western NSW has succeeded with the NSW budget allocating almost $10 million for equipment.

image.altText: Two men and a woman wearing hospital scrubs, standing at operating table performing surgery

items.title: Government department apologises for accidentally underpaying staff

children.content: A Tasmanian government department will rectify the underpayment of more than 900 current and former staff members because it misunderstood the award.

image.altText: A man in a white shirt and tie.

items.title: Animals 'disappearing' from the wild as more people seek exotic pets

children.content: Australian and international authorities are "struggling" to keep up with regulating and policing the exotic pet market as demand surges.

gallery.altText: A meerkat on a lead wearing a woolly jumper.

thumbnail.altText: A meerkat on a lead wearing a woolly jumper.

items.title: Adelaide man 'not criminally responsible' for housemate's murder, court finds

children.content: An Adelaide court has found Kristopher Robert Steele fatally stabbed his housemate, but is "not criminally responsible due to mental incompetence".

image.altText: Police in a street at Cowandilla.

items.title: Angus Taylor refuses to commit to a multicultural Australia

children.content: The opposition leader, pressed multiple times on whether his party still supported multiculturalism or endorsed One Nation leader Pauline Hanson's vision of a "monoculture", refused to commit to either.

image.altText: A man speaks.

items.title: Savannah Guthrie says family in 'agony' amid ransom note reports

children.content: NBC's Today show host Savannah Guthrie makes an emotional appeal to viewers to come forward with any information about her missing mother.

gallery.altText: A tired-looking woman clasps her hands to her heart.

thumbnail.altText: A tired-looking woman clasps her hands to her heart.

items.title: Injury mars Australia's crushing T20 World Cup win over Pakistan

children.content: Australia cruises to a comfortable 113-run victory over Pakistan at Headingley, but there may be concern over a finger injury to one of their stars.

image.altText: Beth Mooney receives treatment during Australia's Women's T20 World Cup match against Pakistan.

items.title: Aussies produce mixed results in Wimbledon qualifying

children.content: Emerson Jones and Storm Hunter advance to the second round of Wimbledon's qualifying stage, but some of their fellow Australians are not as fortunate.

image.altText: Emerson Jones serves during the junior tournament at Wimbledon in 2025.

items.title: Councillor vows to keep working despite suspension for misconduct

children.content: Ashleigh Vandenberg, a councillor in Melbourne's outer-west, plans to continue in her role, despite being handed a two-month suspension for misconduct.
